time. We have to enquire whether we can fix the approximate
measure of progression.
As we advance, we are carrying with us the zodiacal pat
tern found at the moment of our birth. There should then be a
corresponding advance in every cusp and in the position of
each planet. The whole zodiac symbolised as the nativity af
fects us; and consequently the zodiac in its entirety represents
the maximum possible duration of a man's life. When we
divide the 360 degrees of the zodiac by this maximum dura
tion, we may also be able to get the average arc per year. And
this very complicated job can be solved ifwe have a scientifi
cally accurate method to determine the longevity of any nativ
ity. But then new problems may crop up. And we can only

104 Rectification of Birth Time
experiment with the system of directions, before we can arrive
at any certain measure. Now we have, for good or bad, the
Vimshottari system which gives us a maximum of 1,;!0 years.
